⚡ Single trigger: KC_01 fires full CRITICAL alert at storage <50% Nov, but ONLY armed Oct 1 – Apr 30. During May–Sep injection season it is suppressed regardless of score. NGRI = KC_01×0.25 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.20 + KC_05×0.10

Kill Condition Suppressions & Arm Logic

⚡ KC_01 Seasonal Arm Logic — EU Gas Storage Single Trigger

KC_01 is the most consequential kill condition in the Natural Gas domain. It fires a full CRITICAL alert if EU storage falls below 50% in November — the minimum level to prevent supply rationing through a standard European winter.

SUPPRESSED: May 1 – Sep 30 (summer injection season). During these months, low storage readings are expected and normal. Firing KC_01 in summer would be a false positive — storage filling is the entire point of this period.

ARMED: Oct 1 – Apr 30 (winter heating season). The clock starts ticking. If storage drops below 50% by November, the single trigger fires immediately regardless of composite NGRI score.

KC_02 Sustained Spike Requirement — TTF Price

KC_02 requires 5+ consecutive trading days with TTF above €80/MWh to trigger. Single-day spikes (weather, weekend spreads) are suppressed. This prevents LNG portfolio disruption from transient volatility. A 5-day sustained breach implies structural supply failure, not spot noise.

Kill ConditionArm WindowSuppression LogicCurrent State
KC_01 — EU Storage Oct 1 – Apr 30 Suppressed May–Sep (injection season) SUPPRESSED — Jul 2026
KC_02 — TTF Spike Year-round 5+ days sustained >€80/MWh required INACTIVE — TTF €32
KC_03 — LNG Disruption Year-round None — any confirmed force majeure fires immediately INACTIVE — No disruption
KC_04 — Henry Hub Year-round None — NOAA winter forecast amplifies signal INACTIVE — HH $2.80
KC_05 — JKM Premium Year-round None — JKM-TTF spread >250% = demand squeeze INACTIVE — Spread normal

US Stock Impact | Baseline NGRI 0.2800 NORMAL
StockTickerDirectionPrimary ChannelRationale
Cheniere EnergyLNG 🟢 BULLISH eu_gas_crisis + lng_supply_disruption Largest US LNG exporter. EU gas crisis = higher spot cargoes. KC_01 trigger = premium pricing.
Sempra EnergySRE 🟢 BULLISH lng_supply_disruption Port Arthur LNG operator. US export expansion directly benefits SRE LNG infrastructure segment.
ConocoPhillipsCOP 🟢 BULLISH us_domestic_gas + eu_gas_crisis Major LNG equity offtake. Benefits from Henry Hub upside and global LNG demand surge.
Antero ResourcesAR 🟢 BULLISH us_domestic_gas Pure-play Marcellus dry gas producer. KC_04 trigger (HH >$6) = direct revenue uplift.
EQT CorporationEQT 🟢 BULLISH us_domestic_gas Largest US natural gas producer. Appalachian basin leverage to Henry Hub price spikes.
GLD (Gold ETF)GLD 🟢 BULLISH safe_haven_flows Energy crisis = inflation + safe haven premium. European energy shock historically lifts gold.
iShares 20Y TreasuryTLT 🟢 BULLISH safe_haven_flows Flight to safety on European energy disruption. Demand destruction = deflationary offset = bonds bid.
NextEra EnergyNEE 🔴 BEARISH us_domestic_gas Gas-fired generation cost input. Rising Henry Hub = higher fuel cost for NEE gas power fleet.
BASF (ADR/ETF)BASFY 🔴 BEARISH eu_gas_crisis EU largest industrial gas consumer. TTF spike = direct feedstock + energy cost pressure. KC_01 = production cuts.
BorgWarnerBWA 🔴 BEARISH eu_gas_crisis EU auto supplier. Energy cost pressures European manufacturing margins. Demand destruction risk.
KC_01 Flip Scenario: If KC_01 fires (EU storage <50% Nov) — LNG, SRE, COP, AR, EQT become CRITICAL BULLISH (extreme gas price premium). NEE, BASF, BWA accelerate to CRITICAL BEARISH. GLD and TLT surge as European demand destruction confirms stagflation.
Q3 2026 Outlook — Three-Month Signal Calendar
Three conditions that sustain the positive case through Q3:
🟢 EU storage continues injecting at ≥+5%/month toward 90% Nov target
🟢 US LNG trains operational — no force majeure or Gulf hurricane disruption
🟢 NOAA Q4 forecast neutral-to-warm (suppresses KC_01 arm urgency)
All three green → NGRI stays NORMAL through September.

KC_01 EU Storage single trigger (Oct–Apr) is the highest-leverage seasonal signal in CivilisationOS. A European gas crisis fires simultaneously across energy, industrial, and safe-haven domains. Cross-domain: OPEC_OIL_CANARY, RARE_EARTH_CANARY amplified by concurrent gas stress.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2300  NORMAL

Natural Gas / LNG Monitor operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Hormuz (moderate dependency, weight 0.30); Malacca Strait (moderate dependency, weight 0.25); Bab El Mandeb (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include lng regasification, gas storage infra.

Primary exports — liquefied natural gas, pipeline gas, lng shipping — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2300 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Strait of Hormuz closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0800  NORMAL

Natural Gas / LNG Monitor is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is energy price volatility and LNG supply-demand imbalance. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As an emerging market, Global real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Global are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0800 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2500  WATCH

Natural Gas / LNG Monitor faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Tropical Cyclone, Extreme Cold.

The insurance market in Global is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2500 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Tropical Cyclone event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
